data.description:
Run the ADR-0036-mandated version-segmented threshold replay for the plan-v2 impact model ("calibration 3") and adjudicate current blocking thresholds against it. Calibration 2 (2026-07-08) lowered six criteria 0.70->0.60 adjudicated against the OLD mean impact model; the plan-v2 severity-first MAX model with the 0.85 hard floor shipped the same day and has never been replayed. Block rate rose ~25% -> 52-67% immediately after. This ticket produces the evidence base; threshold changes are applied here only if the replay warrants them.

## What
- Run `docs/experiments/calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py --impact-model-version plan-v2` against the live sidecar corpus (verify first, via a corpus scan, what `impact_model_version` tag value plan-v2 sidecars actually carry, and that the segmented corpus is non-trivial; record the segment size vs total).
- Record the full replay output (corpus size, per-criterion table, proposal) as a comment on this ticket.
- Adjudicate: for each blocking-tier criterion (current tiers 0.60 / 0.70 T1,T8,G1G2 / 0.75 E4), compare the replay proposal to the current threshold and decide keep/raise/lower with rationale, honoring the MIN_N=25 low-data deferral.
- Apply warranted changes to the criteria registry thresholds (src/rebar/llm/criteria/) citing the replay evidence; if no change is warranted, record the no-change decision with rationale.
- Record findings relevant to the ac_unverifiable hard-floor split on the dependent story (item 2) so its design starts from measured data.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A comment on this ticket records the segmented replay's full output: the segment's sidecar/ticket/finding counts (and the skipped remainder), the per-criterion table, and the script's proposal block.
- [ ] A comment records the adjudication: every blocking-tier criterion mapped from current threshold to a keep/raise/lower decision with rationale grounded in the replay signals (validity, rev_rr, p90, fire rate, n vs MIN_N).
- [ ] Either warranted threshold changes are landed in the criteria registry (change merged via Gerrit) OR a no-change decision is recorded in the adjudication comment - one of the two, explicitly.
- [ ] A comment on the dependent floor-split story records the replay/corpus evidence about ac_unverifiable hard-floor fires (how often the 0.85 floor drove the block, missing-oracle vs under-specified-oracle mix if derivable).
data.title: Calibration 3: plan-v2 segmented threshold replay + adjudication (ADR-0036)
